title: Desktop Menu Action Registry — Dynamic Menu Actions
consumer_purpose: Feature modules register menu actions and handlers, replacing the monolithic hardcoded DESKTOP_MENU and the switch-based action dispatch in desktop-menu-actions.ts
claim_atoms: MenuActionRegistry class stores menu sections + action handlers; feature modules (menu-app.ts

menu-help.ts) register their sections; menu.ts builds from registry; desktop-menu-actions.ts delegates to registry; Windows React menu unchanged (DESKTOP_MENU kept in @opencode-ai/app for backward compat)
content:
# Lane HC-002: Dynamic Menu Actions

## Problem
The desktop menu actions are hardcoded in `packages/app/src/desktop-menu.ts` (the DESKTOP_MENU constant) and dispatched via a big switch in `packages/desktop/src/main/desktop-menu-actions.ts`. Feature modules cannot declare their own menu actions without modifying these two files.

## Solution
Create a `MenuActionRegistry` where feature modules register menu sections and action handlers, mirroring how IPC handlers were decomposed in HC-001.

## Files

### New files
1. `packages/desktop/src/main/menu-action-registry.ts` — Core registry class with add(), on(), build(), execute()
2. `packages/desktop/src/main/menu-app.ts` — Application menu (macOS): about, updates, settings, restart, quit. Registers app.checkForUpdates and app.relaunch handlers
3. `packages/desktop/src/main/menu-view-window.ts` — Edit + View + Window menus: all webContents-level operations (undo/redo/clipboard/devtools/zoom/fullscreen) and window operations (new/close/minimize/maximize)
4. `packages/desktop/src/main/menu-help.ts` — Help menu: docs, support, plugin manager, feedback, bug report

### Modified files
5. `packages/desktop/src/main/menu.ts` — Build from registry instead of importing DESKTOP_MENU
6. `packages/desktop/src/main/desktop-menu-actions.ts` — Delegate to the registry; becomes a thin forwarder
7. `packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts` — No changes needed; createMenu() signature unchanged

### Unchanged
- `packages/app/src/desktop-menu.ts` — Types kept; DESKTOP_MENU kept for Windows React menu (windows-app-menu.tsx)
- `packages/app/src/components/windows-app-menu.tsx` — Unchanged, uses DESKTOP_MENU directly

## Design

### MenuActionRegistry class
```
add(menu: DesktopMenu)        → register a top-level menu section
on(action, handler)           → register an action handler
build(): DesktopMenu[]        → get all registered sections
execute(action, win, ...args) → dispatch an action to its handler (returns false if unhandled)
```

### Feature module pattern (matching IPC decomposition)
Each module exports a `register*MenuActions(registry, deps?)` function that calls:
- `registry.add(menuSection)` — for menu structure
- `registry.on(action, handler)` — for action execution

### Modified nativeItem()
Click handlers for `action` entries now call `registry.execute()` instead of `runDesktopMenuAction()`. The `command` and `href` handlers remain unchanged.

### runDesktopMenuAction()
Becomes a thin forwarder to the registry, keeping backward compatibility with the IPC path (ipc-window.ts calls it from renderer requests).
